tools.description: Switch to a branch, creating the worktree and tmux window if needed. Handles all cases: switches to an existing tmux window, reopens a closed window, fetches a remote branch, or creates a new branch from the base. Use when the user wants to work on any branch, whether it already exists or is brand new. The branch name typically follows a convention like 'feature/ABC-1234', 'defect/BCDE-5678', or 'spike/try-redis'.

base.description: Base branch to fork from when creating a new branch. Defaults to the configured base (usually 'main'). Ignored if the branch already exists.

sbx.description: Also create a sandbox with port publishing and services when creating a new branch. Requires sbx config. Ignored if the branch already exists.

tools.name: worktree_switch

tools.description: Remove a git worktree and close its tmux window. Use when the user is done with a branch or wants to clean up a workspace. DESTRUCTIVE: prune=true deletes the local git branch permanently. Remote branch deletion requires both prune=true and confirm_remote_delete=true. The configured base branch (e.g. 'main', 'develop') cannot be pruned.

branch.description: Branch name of the worktree to remove (e.g. 'feature/ABC-1234')

confirm_remote_delete.description: If true (and prune is also true), also delete the remote branch on origin. Default: false. Use only when the work is fully merged.

prune.description: If true, delete the local git branch. Default: false.

tools.name: worktree_remove

tools.description: List all active git worktrees in the current repository with their tmux window status. Use when the user wants to see what branches they have checked out, what workspaces are active, or get an overview of their current work.
tools.name: worktree_list

tools.description: Create a sandbox (dev container) for a branch. Builds the sandbox image from the project template if needed, then starts a container with port publishing and services. Use when the user wants an isolated development environment for a branch.

branch.description: Branch name to create the sandbox for (e.g. 'feature/ABC-1234')

tools.name: sandbox_new

tools.description: Destroy a sandbox (dev container) for a branch. Stops and removes the container. Use when the user is done with an isolated development environment and wants to free resources.

branch.description: Branch name whose sandbox should be removed (e.g. 'feature/ABC-1234')

tools.name: sandbox_remove

tools.description: List all sandboxes in the current project with their status and published ports. Use when the user wants to see which branches have active sandboxes or check sandbox health.
tools.name: sandbox_list

tools.description: Build and load the sandbox template image for the current project. Use when the user wants to prepare or update the base image that sandboxes are created from.
tools.name: sandbox_build
